commit.message:
Add BFloat16 support via Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and BFloat16s extension (#1042)

* Add Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and rules

- Tangent space of BFloat16 is BFloat16 itself (mirrors IEEEFloat treatment)
- Implements all tangent/fdata/rdata infrastructure methods
- Conversion rules: BFloat16 <-> Float32/Float64
- Math rules: sqrt, cbrt, exp*, log*, trig, hyperbolic, hypot, ^, atan2, max, min, abs, eps, nextfloat, prevfloat
- All code guarded by `@static if isdefined(Core, :BFloat16)` (Julia >= 1.11)
- Wires into CI test matrix as 'rules/bfloat16'

Closes #935

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move BFloat16 rules to MooncakeBFloat16sExt

- src/rules/bfloat16.jl now only contains type-level methods
  (tangent_type, fdata_type, rdata_type, can_produce_zero_rdata_from_type, etc.)
  that work without BFloat16s.jl loaded
- ext/MooncakeBFloat16sExt.jl contains arithmetic infrastructure
  (zero_tangent_internal, _scale_internal, _dot_internal, etc.) and all
  conversion/math rules — using natural BFloat16 arithmetic without
  explicit Float32 intermediates
- test/ext/bfloat16s/ provides the extension test (replaces rules/bfloat16 group)
- Project.toml: BFloat16s added to [weakdeps], [extensions], and [compat]
- CI: rules/bfloat16 matrix entry replaced with {test_type: ext, label: bfloat16s}

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment on _dot_internal return type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment on zero_tangent_internal placement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard bfloat16s test for platforms where BFloat16s.BFloat16 !== Core.BFloat16

On aarch64 with LLVM < 19, BFloat16s.jl defines its own primitive type and
Core.BFloat16 has no arithmetic. Tests are skipped there and run on x86_64
(LLVM >= 15) where BFloat16s.BFloat16 === Core.BFloat16.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use #! format: off in bfloat16.jl to avoid indentation inside @static if

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix BFloat16 extension rules and tests

- Fix sin/cos frules/rrules: use separate sin(x)+cos(x) calls instead of
  sincos(x) which infinitely recurses in Julia 1.12 for BFloat16
- Fix sinpi/cospi frules/rrules: use sinpi(x)/cospi(x) directly instead of
  sincospi(x) which returns Float64 and mismatches primal for edge cases
- Add Base.sinpi/cospi overloads for BFloat16 returning correct BFloat16 values
- Add Base.atan(y::P, x::P) overload since two-arg atan is not defined for
  BFloat16 in Julia 1.12
- Add JET and AllocCheck to test deps (required by test_tangent_interface)
- Use FD-friendly test values in [0.25, 0.5) BFloat16 range where finite
  differences are accurate given BFloat16's limited precision
- Add atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 tolerances to test_rule calls to accommodate
  BFloat16 quantization artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Drop sinpi, cospi, and two-arg atan rules and tests

These functions have broken or inconsistent behaviour for BFloat16 inputs
in Julia 1.12 (sinpi/cospi return Float64, atan(::BF16,::BF16) is missing),
so the rules and their test cases are removed rather than worked around.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten BFloat16 test tolerances to atol=0.2, rtol=0.4

The previous atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 were overly loose. Analysis of BFloat16 FD
behaviour guides the new values:

- rtol=0.4 covers the ~24% relative snap error when test values are in the
  [0.125, 0.25) BFloat16 range (spacing 0.000977), where the ε=1e-2
  perturbation rounds to exactly one grid step (0.000977) vs the intended
  ~0.00129, giving ẋ_fd/ẋ ≈ 0.76.

- atol=0.2 covers two edge cases that cannot be fixed by value selection alone:
  · acos(P(0.2)): output ∈ [1,2) with BF16 spacing 0.0078, so ẏ_fd=0 at all
    ε<1e-2; at ε=1e-2 the snap gives |LHS-RHS|=0.159 which needs atol≥0.16.
  · exp10(P(0.249)): chosen so f'(x)≈4.08 just exceeds the 4.0 threshold
    needed for the output perturbation to cross a BF16 boundary, giving a
    non-zero ẏ_fd at ε=1e-2 and |LHS-RHS|≈0.156 < atol=0.2.

All other test values moved to [0.125, 0.25) or [0.2, 0.25) to ensure:
1. Input perturbation is not absorbed (half-spacing 0.000488 < 0.00129)
2. Snap error stays within rtol=0.4
3. rrule tests use normalized tangents (|ẋ|≈1) so ε=1e-2 >> spacing, giving
   accurate FD well within atol=0.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining BFloat16 test tolerances

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard MooncakeBFloat16sExt body with @static if isdefined(Core, :BFloat16)

Core.BFloat16 is only available on Julia >= 1.11. Without the guard the
extension would error on Julia 1.10 LTS when BFloat16s.jl is loaded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Updated conditional check for Core.BFloat16 availability based on Julia version.

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments + fix BFloat16s ext precompilation

- Move can_produce_zero_rdata_from_type into MooncakeBFloat16sExt
- Add nan_tangent_guard to ^ frule/rrule (guards x=0, y<1 → NaN tangents)
- Fix @static if guard to also check BFloat16s.BFloat16 === Core.BFloat16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix NaN guards in BFloat16 ^ frule and rrule

- frule: guard on z instead of tangent(y) for the log term,
  since z*log(_x) = 0*(-Inf) = NaN when _x == 0 regardless of tangent(y)
- rrule: nest nan_tangent_guard(z, ...) inside nan_tangent_guard(dz, ...)
  to guard the z*log(_x) term against the same 0*(-Inf) = NaN case

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Test NaN guard edge cases for BFloat16 ^ frule and rrule

with x=0, y=0.5, where z*log(x) = 0*(-Inf) = NaN without the guard.
Asserts that neither the forward tangent nor the reverse-mode rdata
contains NaN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments on BFloat16 ^ rules

- Add comments explaining why _x^(_y-1) cannot be rewritten as z/_x
  (0/0=NaN vs the correct Inf at the boundary) and why the nested
  nan_tangent_guard is needed (two independent NaN sources)
- Strengthen NaN guard tests: assert exact values (Inf and zero(P))
  rather than just !isnan; add a second frule call with dx=0 to verify
  the y-term guard independently

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix imprecise comments in BFloat16 ^ rules and tests

- Broaden the _x^(_y-1) note: the "= Inf" claim only holds for 0<y<1;
  rewrite as "gives the correct value at the boundary"
- Test: replace "x-term dominates" with "y-term guarded to 0; x-term = Inf"
- Test: "may be Inf" -> "is Inf" with the exact expression for these inputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: reset task id counter (#843)

* feat: add ResetNextTaskId RPC for resetting task ID counters

Admin-only RPC that resets task ID and task DB ID counters in the
embedded database without requiring a ctld restart. Used by the test
framework to reset state between test cases.

- Proto: ResetNextTaskIdRequest/Reply with next_task_id and next_task_db_id
  fields (0 = don't change, >0 = reset to value)
- EmbeddedDbClient: ResetNextTaskId() resets counters in both DB and memory
  (also resets step counters when task ID is reset)
- RPC handler: admin auth via CheckUidIsAdmin, server readiness check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add purge/reset RPCs for all metadata types

Add bulk cleanup capabilities for test framework integration:

- PurgeAllAccounts: post-order subtree deletion, skip ROOT
- PurgeAllQos: skip UNLIMITED default QoS
- PurgeAllWckeys: skip root user's wckeys
- PurgeAllLicenseResources: clear all license/resource entries
- PurgeAllReservations: delete all reservations
- ResetAllPartitionAcls: reload defaults from config or clear all
- ResetNextStepDbId: reset step ID counters independently

Each triggered via "delete ALL --force" CLI pattern or dedicated
reset RPC. Enables skip-ctld-restart test optimization (275/281 pass).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: block account all and reset cert all (#834)

Add RPC to purge all task/step history records from embedded DB
(fixed_db, variable_db, step_fixed_db, step_var_db) without
deleting files. This replaces the unsafe wipe_embedded() approach
which unlinks files while ctld holds open fds — causing data loss
when ctld restarts (new process can't find unlinked files).

Frontend command: ccontrol reset task-history

commit.message:
fix: resolve 77 CodeQL code scanning alerts

iccanalyzer-lite (2 fixes):
- IccHeuristicsRawPost.cpp: restructure LUT tag sig index to prevent
  unsigned subtraction before bounds check (4 buffer-overflow alerts)
- IccImageAnalyzer.cpp: use fstat(fileno(fp)) instead of stat()+fopen()
  to eliminate TOCTOU race condition (1 alert)

CFL fuzzers (6 fixes):
- fuzz_utils.h: cast data bytes to uint32_t before left-shift to prevent
  signed-shift-overflow UB (4 alerts)
- icc_toxml_fuzzer.cpp, icc_dump_fuzzer.cpp: use open(O_CREAT, 0600)
  instead of fopen("wb") to prevent world-writable temp files (2 alerts)
- icc_fromcube_fuzzer.cpp: copy interior pointer values to local arrays
  to fix use-after-expired-lifetime (9 alerts), use memcmp for float
  equality matching upstream exact-bit semantics (8 alerts)
- icc_specsep_fuzzer.cpp: use memcmp for TIFF resolution float comparison
  matching upstream exact semantics (2 alerts)
- icc_applynamedcmm_fuzzer.cpp: remove unused pcs and SrcNameBuf
  variables (2 alerts)

MCP server (2 fixes):
- web_ui.py: add resolve()+relative_to() defense-in-depth for path
  traversal prevention in /api/list-xml (1 alert)
- icc_profile_mcp.py: add explanatory comments to except-pass blocks
  for process cleanup and TTL file removal (4 alerts)

Dismissed (42 alerts):
- 32 colorbleed_tools alerts (intentionally unsafe by design)
- 5 upstream iccDEV alerts (not our code)
- 3 CFL XXE alerts (fuzzers intentionally process XML)
- 2 informational alerts (custom query, style)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace head pipes with sed in CWE-400 workflow to prevent SIGPIPE

With set -euo pipefail, 'find tools -type f | head -30' causes SIGPIPE
(exit 141) when >30 files exist because head closes stdin early. This
killed the 'Prepare tools' step before tool binaries could be located,
causing ALL verify jobs to fail on every run.

Replace all '| head -N' with '| sed -n 1,Np' which reads all input
without closing stdin early — SIGPIPE-safe under pipefail.

Fixes: All 5 prior CWE-400 workflow runs failing at 'Prepare tools'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace head -1 with sed -n 1p to avoid SIGPIPE

find | head -1 with set -euo pipefail causes SIGPIPE (exit 141)
when find has multiple results and head closes the pipe early.
sed -n 1p reads all input without early close.

commit.message:
feat: allow email reuse in orgs with updated unique indx on user emails (#853)

**Old indexes (migration 033):**

1. `idx_users_email_native_auth` — `UNIQUE (email) WHERE
external_user_id IS NULL`
2. `idx_users_email_external_user_id_federated` — `UNIQUE (email,
external_user_id) WHERE external_user_id IS NOT NULL`

**New index:**

3. `idx_users_email_auth_source_external_id` — `UNIQUE (email,
auth_source, external_user_id) NULLS NOT DISTINCT`

Let's walk through each scenario:

| Scenario | Old behavior | New behavior | Change? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Two native users, same email | Blocked (index 1) | Blocked (both have
`auth_source=native`, `external_user_id=NULL`) | Same |
| Two orgs, same email | Blocked (index 1, both NULL external_id) |
Blocked (both have `auth_source=organization`, `external_user_id=NULL`)
| Same |
| Native user + org, same email | **Blocked** (index 1, both NULL
external_id) | **Allowed** (different `auth_source`: `native` vs
`organization`) | **Changed** |
| Proxy header user + org, same email | **Blocked** (index 1, both NULL
external_id) | **Allowed** (different `auth_source`: `proxy_header` vs
`organization`) | **Changed** |
| Two federated users, same email, same external_id | Blocked (index 2)
| Blocked (all three columns match) | Same |
| Two federated users, same email, different external_id | Allowed
(index 2, different external_id) | Allowed (different external_id) |
Same |
| Federated user + org, same email | Allowed (different partitions:
index 1 vs 2) | Allowed (different auth_source + external_id) | Same |
| Federated user + native user, same email | Allowed (different
partitions) | Allowed (different auth_source) | Same |
| Two proxy header users, same email | **Blocked** (index 1, both NULL
external_id) | **Blocked** (`NULLS NOT DISTINCT`) | Same |

So the **only behavioral change** is: an org can now share an email with
a native or proxy header user. Everything else stays the same.

One thing to flag though — proxy header users also have
`external_user_id = NULL`. Under the old index, two proxy header users
with the same email were blocked. Under the new index they're still
blocked (`NULLS NOT DISTINCT` + same `auth_source`). That's correct. But
should two proxy header users with the same email actually be allowed?
They could represent different SSO identities. If so, that's a separate
issue — not a regression from this migration.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add exit-code contract tests for iccDEV tools

Origin: Merge defect analysis of 99 iccDEV PRs.
PR #583 silently changed 14 tools from return -1 to return 0
for no-args/usage. This workflow codifies the exit-code contract:
- No-args → exit 0 (usage display)
- Bad input → exit nonzero
- Valid input → exit 0
- ASAN/UBSAN clean on all paths

Catches behavioral regressions that change exit semantics
without updating documentation or downstream scripts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
